comprehension: Why does the Evil Queen ask the mirror 'Who is the fairest of them all?'
What does the huntsman do when he's ordered to kill Snow White?
How does Snow White escape into the forest?

informational.title: Mirrors Through History
informational.topic: How mirrors were made in ancient times, their importance in royal life, and mirror symbolism

31.journal: The Evil Queen was very proud of her beauty. Write about something you're good at and how to be proud without being vain.
31.opinion: Was the huntsman right to disobey the Queen and save Snow White? Explain your thinking.

grammar.example: "Mirror, mirror on the wall, who is the fairest of them all?" asked the Queen.

comprehension: What does the Queen believe when the huntsman returns?
How does Snow White survive alone in the forest?
What does Snow White feel when she's lost in the dark woods?

informational.title: Forests in Medieval Europe
informational.topic: Dark forests in fairy tales, real dangers in medieval woods, and why forests were feared

32.journal: Snow White was brave even though she was scared. Write about a time you were brave.
32.opinion: Should Snow White have trusted the huntsman? Why or why not?

grammar.example: The dark, frightening forest surrounded the lost princess.

comprehension: What does Snow White find in the forest?
How many dwarfs live in the little cottage?
What deal does Snow White make with the dwarfs?

informational.title: Life in Small Cottages
informational.topic: How people lived in small homes in the past, daily chores, and simple living

33.journal: The dwarfs welcomed Snow White into their home. Write about what makes a house feel like a home.
33.opinion: Was it fair for the dwarfs to ask Snow White to cook and clean in exchange for shelter?

grammar.example: Seven little dwarfs lived in the cottage.

comprehension: What jobs do the seven dwarfs have?
Why do the dwarfs warn Snow White to be careful?
How does Snow White spend her days with the dwarfs?

informational.title: Mining and Miners in History
informational.topic: What miners did, precious gems and metals, and the dangerous work of mining

34.journal: Each dwarf had a different personality. Describe what makes you unique and special.
34.opinion: Is it better to live alone in a big castle or with friends in a small cottage?

grammar.example: The dwarfs were small, hardworking, and kind.

comprehension: How does the Queen discover Snow White is still alive?
What disguises does the Evil Queen use?
What tricks does the Queen try on Snow White?

informational.title: Disguises and Deception
informational.topic: How disguises work, why people use costumes, and recognizing danger

36.journal: Snow White trusted strangers even though she was warned. Write about when it's okay to trust people and when you should be careful.
36.opinion: Why did Snow White keep falling for the Queen's tricks? Should she have been more careful?

grammar.example: Because the Queen was jealous, she disguised herself to harm Snow White.

comprehension: What does the Queen bring to Snow White this time?
Why does Snow White eat the poisoned apple?
What happens to Snow White after she takes a bite?

informational.title: Poison and Medicine in History
informational.topic: How people used plants for healing and harm, recognizing danger, and being cautious

37.journal: Sometimes things that look beautiful can be dangerous. Write about a time when something wasn't what it seemed.
37.opinion: Could Snow White's story have ended differently if she had listened to the dwarfs' warnings?

grammar.example: Snow White reached for the beautiful, deadly apple.

comprehension: What do the dwarfs do when they find Snow White?
How does the Prince discover Snow White?
How is Snow White brought back to life?

informational.title: Royal Life and Princes
informational.topic: What princes did in medieval times, royal weddings, and castle life

38.journal: The story has a happy ending. Write about what 'happily ever after' means to you.
38.opinion: Do you think the Evil Queen got what she deserved at the end? Why or why not?

grammar.example: True love's power broke the evil spell at last.

comprehension: How does Cinderella's stepfamily treat her?
What chores does Cinderella have to do every day?
Why is Cinderella called by that name?

informational.title: Stepmothers in Fairy Tales
informational.topic: Why stepmothers are often villains in stories, blended families, and treating others fairly

39.journal: Cinderella stayed kind even when others were cruel. Write about how you can be kind to people who aren't kind to you.
39.opinion: Should Cinderella have stood up to her stepfamily, or was it better to stay quiet and be patient?

grammar.example: The stepsisters were cruel, but Cinderella remained gentle and good.

comprehension: What does Cinderella plant at her mother's grave?
How does the hazel tree help Cinderella?
What magical help does Cinderella receive?

informational.title: Trees in Folklore and Legend
informational.topic: Why trees are magical in stories, wishing trees, and the symbolism of growing things

41.journal: Cinderella's tears and wishes made magic happen. Write about something you wish for and why it's important to you.
41.opinion: Is it better to get help from magic or to solve problems on your own?

grammar.example: Cinderella wished with all her heart for a way to go to the ball.

comprehension: What does Cinderella wear to the ball?
How does the Prince react when he sees Cinderella?
Why does Cinderella have to leave at midnight?

informational.title: Royal Balls and Dancing
informational.topic: Fancy parties in castles, ballroom dancing, and social events in history

42.journal: Cinderella got to experience one magical night. Describe a special event or celebration you remember.
42.opinion: Why do you think the Prince was so interested in Cinderella and not the other girls?

grammar.example: Cinderella had to leave before midnight struck.

comprehension: Does Cinderella go to the ball a second night?
What does the Prince do to try to find Cinderella?
What does Cinderella leave behind when she runs away?

informational.title: Shoes Through History
informational.topic: Different types of shoes, glass slippers in stories, and why shoes were important status symbols

43.journal: The Prince searched for Cinderella even though he didn't know her name. Write about determination and not giving up.
43.opinion: Was it smart for the Prince to search for Cinderella using only a shoe? Could that plan have failed?

grammar.example: Cinderella ran down the stairs, losing her slipper as the clock began to chime.

comprehension: How does the Prince find Cinderella?
What do the stepsisters try to do?
How does everyone know Cinderella is the true princess?

informational.title: Truth and Honesty
informational.topic: Why telling the truth matters, consequences of lying, and integrity

44.journal: The stepsisters tried to fake being Cinderella. Write about why it's important to be yourself.
44.opinion: Do you think Cinderella should forgive her stepfamily or never speak to them again?

grammar.example: When the slipper fit perfectly, everyone knew the truth.

comprehension: What happens at Cinderella's wedding?
How are the stepsisters punished?
What does Cinderella's happy ending look like?

informational.title: Justice and Consequences
informational.topic: Fairness, punishment for wrongdoing, and karma in stories

46.journal: Cinderella's kindness was rewarded in the end. Write about a time when doing the right thing led to something good.
46.opinion: Was the punishment of the stepsisters too harsh, just right, or too gentle? Defend your answer.

grammar.example: Cinderella and the Prince lived happily ever after in the castle.

comprehension: Why are Hansel and Gretel's family so poor?
What is the stepmother's terrible plan?
How does the father feel about the plan?

informational.title: Famine and Hunger in History
informational.topic: What causes famines, how people survived hard times, and food scarcity

47.journal: Hansel and Gretel's family faced a very difficult choice. Write about a time when you had to make a hard decision.
47.opinion: Was the stepmother's plan to abandon the children ever justified, even during a famine?

grammar.example: The family had no food, and they didn't know how to survive.

comprehension: How does Hansel save himself and Gretel the first time?
What do the white pebbles do in the moonlight?
Do the children make it back home?

informational.title: Navigation and Finding Your Way
informational.topic: How people navigate without technology, using nature's clues, and not getting lost

48.journal: Hansel was clever and made a plan. Write about a time when you solved a problem in a creative way.
48.opinion: Was Hansel brave or just clever? What's the difference between the two?

grammar.example: Hansel secretly dropped white pebbles to mark the path home.

comprehension: Why can't Hansel collect pebbles the second time?
What does Hansel use instead to mark the trail?
What happens to the breadcrumb trail?

informational.title: Birds and Forest Animals
informational.topic: What birds eat, animals that live in forests, and nature's food chain

49.journal: Hansel's plan didn't work the second time. Write about a time when something didn't go as planned and what you did.
49.opinion: Should Hansel have tried a different plan for marking the path? What else could he have used?

grammar.example: Although Hansel dropped breadcrumbs, the birds ate them all.

comprehension: How do Hansel and Gretel feel when they realize they're lost?
How long do they wander in the forest?
What keeps them going even though they're scared?

informational.title: Survival in the Wilderness
informational.topic: What to do if you're lost, finding food and water, and staying safe outdoors

51.journal: Being lost is frightening. Write about what you would do if you were lost and how you would stay brave.
51.opinion: Is it better to stay in one place when lost or to keep moving to find help?

grammar.example: Though they were terrified, the children held hands and walked on.

comprehension: What amazing thing do Hansel and Gretel discover?
What is the house made of?
Why do the children start eating the house?

informational.title: Gingerbread and Sweet Treats
informational.topic: History of gingerbread houses, candy in fairy tales, and temptation

52.journal: The gingerbread house seemed too good to be true. Write about something that seemed perfect but turned out to be a trick.
52.opinion: Should Hansel and Gretel have been more suspicious of a house made of candy?

grammar.example: The house was so delicious that the children couldn't resist.

comprehension: Who lives in the gingerbread house?
How does the witch trick the children?
What is the witch's evil plan?

informational.title: Witches in Fairy Tales
informational.topic: Why witches appear in stories, historical witch hunts, and recognizing danger

53.journal: The witch pretended to be kind at first. Write about how to tell if someone is truly kind or just pretending.
53.opinion: Could Hansel and Gretel have escaped before being captured? What should they have done differently?

grammar.example: The sweet old woman was actually a wicked witch!

comprehension: Why does the witch put Hansel in a cage?
What does the witch make Gretel do?
How does Hansel trick the witch?

informational.title: Cleverness and Outsmarting Enemies
informational.topic: Using your brain to solve problems, trickery in stories, and staying calm under pressure

54.journal: Hansel used his mind to fool the witch. Write about why being smart is just as important as being strong.
54.opinion: Was it okay for Hansel to lie and trick the witch? When is it okay to deceive someone?

grammar.example: Each day, Hansel held out a small bone instead of his finger.

comprehension: How does Gretel help save herself and Hansel?
What clever plan does Gretel come up with?
How does Gretel trick the witch?

informational.title: Ovens and Cooking in Old Times
informational.topic: How people cooked before modern stoves, bread-baking, and kitchen life long ago

56.journal: Gretel had to be brave and think fast. Write about a time you had to be quick-thinking or clever.
56.opinion: Who was braver: Hansel for his tricks or Gretel for defeating the witch? Explain your choice.

grammar.example: Gretel pretended she didn't know how, tricking the witch into showing her.

comprehension: What happens to the witch?
How do Hansel and Gretel feel after defeating the witch?
What do the children do after they're free?

informational.title: Good Triumphing Over Evil
informational.topic: Why fairy tales have happy endings, justice in stories, and overcoming evil

57.journal: Hansel and Gretel won by working together. Write about why teamwork is important.
57.opinion: Did the witch deserve what happened to her? Is there ever a reason to feel sorry for villains?

grammar.example: The wicked witch perished, and the children were finally safe!

comprehension: What do Hansel and Gretel find in the witch's house?
What do they take with them?
How will the treasure help their family?

informational.title: Treasure and Riches
informational.topic: Pearls, jewels, and precious gems, what made people rich in the past, and wealth in stories

58.journal: The children found treasure that would help their family. Write about something valuable that isn't money.
58.opinion: Was it right for Hansel and Gretel to take the witch's treasure? Does it matter that she was evil?

grammar.example: They gasped when they saw the glittering jewels and gold!

comprehension: How do Hansel and Gretel finally get home?
Who is there to greet them when they return?
How does the story end?

informational.title: Family Reunions and Forgiveness
informational.topic: The importance of family, reconciliation, and second chances

59.journal: Hansel and Gretel came home changed by their adventure. Write about an experience that changed you or taught you something important.
59.opinion: Should Hansel and Gretel forgive their father for abandoning them? Why or why not?

grammar.example: Their father wept with joy, and they all lived happily together at last.
